rulings.Case Title: DIANE BECK VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 19, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant D. Zelinsky & Sons, Inc.¿s motion to dismiss is denied. The court does not find proper authority to grant defendant¿s motion pursuant to C.C.P. sections 128(a) or 581(m). The motion is denied without prejudice to being made again before the trial judge. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ELVIN GAZZANO VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 19, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3, Defendant FDCC California, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. The declarations of Mr. Shortall and Mr. Ay create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (insulation) attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff¿s causes of action for strict liability and false representation and claim for punitive damages is off calendar. A dismissal is on file as to such claims. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD MELVIN UHR et al VS. AMERICAN LAFRANCE LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Certainteed Corporations Motion For A Change Of Venue From San Francisco County To Sonoma County
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 19, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2, Defendant CertainTeed Corporation¿s motion to change venue from San Francisco County to Sonoma County is denied. Defendant failed to satisfy its burden of demonstrating that either San Francico County is not the proper court and/or that transferring the case to Sonoma County would promote convenience of witnesses and the ends of justice as required by C.C.P. section 397. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
